title: Healthcare Federated Learning — FL + TEE + DP (S-01 pattern)
description: Cross-institutional federated training for clinical imaging or screening models, following the NVIDIA FLARE / NHS FLIP pattern. Local data never leaves institution; aggregation runs in a hardware-isolated enclave; DP bounds statistical disclosure from the released model.

pet_components.role: local data retention — raw clinical data stays on institution devices; only model updates leave the site
pet_components.parameter_notes: Non-IID data distribution across sites degrades convergence; convergence monitoring required per round.

pet_components.role: hardware isolation of aggregation server — enclave protects intermediate model state from cloud operator

parameters.side_channel_mitigations: memory encryption enabled; no hyperthreading on enclave vCPUs

pet_components.parameter_notes: Enclave measurement must be pinned and verified at each aggregation round start.

pet_components.role: formal output disclosure bound — noise added during aggregation to limit individual record leakage from the released model
pet_components.parameter_notes: ε=4.0 is a pragmatic choice for healthcare imaging utility; tighter values degrade AUROC significantly. Composition tracked across all 100 training rounds via RDP accountant.

trust_model.summary: Participating institutions are semi-trusted data holders. The aggregation server is operated by a cloud provider and is considered honest-but-curious at the OS/hypervisor level — the TEE isolates it from the aggregation computation. The released model is considered public output and must satisfy the DP bound. External analysts are untrusted.

threat_actors.capability: can observe all network traffic and OS-level memory outside the enclave
threat_actors.mitigated_by: TEE — enclave isolates aggregation computation from the host OS
threat_actors.residual_risk: Side-channel attacks (Spectre-class) remain a residual risk; enclave memory limits may require model partitioning

threat_actors.capability: can observe individual model updates from participating institutions before summation
threat_actors.mitigated_by: TEE — intermediate update state is inside the enclave; DP bounds what the released model reveals
threat_actors.residual_risk: Without MPC secure aggregation (S-03 pattern), the coordinator inside the enclave still sees individual updates. TEE attestation does not prevent this — only MPC addition eliminates it.

threat_actors.capability: can perform membership inference and model inversion attacks on the released model
threat_actors.mitigated_by: DP — formal bound (ε=4.0) on individual record leakage from the model
threat_actors.residual_risk: ε=4.0 provides moderate protection; tight membership inference attacks remain partially feasible at this epsilon value

out_of_scope_threats: Byzantine poisoning attacks from malicious participating institutions
Data quality and demographic bias issues within participating sites
Supply-chain compromise of the NVIDIA FLARE framework

required_artefacts.artefact: attestation report and enclave measurement (TEE layer)
required_artefacts.notes: Generated per aggregation round; pinned measurement hash stored in change-control log

required_artefacts.artefact: privacy accountant and parameter manifest (DP layer)
required_artefacts.notes: RDP accountant tracks epsilon spend across all 100 rounds; total budget ε=4.0 not exceeded

required_artefacts.artefact: training protocol and aggregation specification (FL layer)

required_artefacts.notes: Release process under review with clinical governance board; target Q3 2026

required_artefacts.artefact: side-channel mitigation declaration for the aggregation enclave

assurance_targets.maturity_justification: Workflow is repeatable and documented. Stage 3 blocked by absence of formal release log and pending clinical governance board sign-off on output clearance process.

governance_controls.access_control: Institutional DUA (Data Use Agreement) required per participating site; access to aggregated model gated on clinical governance board approval
governance_controls.change_control: Parameter changes (epsilon, clipping norm, aggregation strategy) require change-control log entry and governance review

frameworks_applied.notes: IDENTIFY and GOVERN functions covered; COMMUNICATE function under development

applicable_regulations.gap_notes: Art. 9 basis documentation for medical research (Art. 9(2)(j)) in progress

standards_alignment.notes: RDP accounting documented; parameter governance review process not yet formalised

tooling_candidates.name: NVIDIA FLARE
tooling_candidates.notes: Used by NHS FLIP (King's College London) and multiple clinical FL deployments

tooling_candidates.name: Google dp_accounting
tooling_candidates.notes: RDP and PRV accountants; recommended for FL+DP composition tracking

residual_risks.risk: Coordinator inside TEE sees individual institution updates before summation — TEE does not provide MPC-grade isolation of individual contributions
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Upgrade to S-03 pattern (FL + MPC + DP) to eliminate coordinator visibility of individual updates

residual_risks.risk: ε=4.0 provides only moderate membership inference protection; tight attacks remain partially feasible
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Reduce epsilon for subsequent model generations as utility benchmarks allow; document accepted risk in clinical governance sign-off

residual_risks.risk: Non-IID data distribution across institutions may degrade model utility disproportionately for minority site distributions
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Per-site utility evaluation and fairness analysis as part of convergence reporting

utility_evidence.notes: 4-point utility degradation at ε=4.0 vs. non-private centralised baseline. Acceptable per clinical governance assessment for this use case.

change_log.change: Initial card created as YAPS example
change_log.author: Privacy Explorer
